messages.text: morning team — our brewline dashboard is showing every hopper as offline since 8am. we've had no orders ingested. is it on your end or ours?

text_entities.text: morning team — our brewline dashboard is showing every hopper as offline since 8am. we've had no orders ingested. is it on your end or ours?

messages.text: hi olivia — we see the same on our side, investigating. apologies. I'll have a status update in 15.

text_entities.text: hi olivia — we see the same on our side, investigating. apologies. I'll have a status update in 15.

messages.text: update: it's on us. our queue worker hit a timeout cascade at 7:55am. we're rolling a fix now, eta 20 minutes to recovery.

text_entities.text: update: it's on us. our queue worker hit a timeout cascade at 7:55am. we're rolling a fix now, eta 20 minutes to recovery.

messages.text: ok thanks. can you confirm: any data was lost? we have 4 retail locations that have been pushing orders this morning.

text_entities.text: ok thanks. can you confirm: any data was lost? we have 4 retail locations that have been pushing orders this morning.

messages.text: no data loss — orders were buffered on the device side. they'll sync as soon as the queue is healthy.

text_entities.text: no data loss — orders were buffered on the device side. they'll sync as soon as the queue is healthy.

messages.text: queue is healthy, sync is in progress. seeing 1,847 buffered orders flowing through now.

text_entities.text: queue is healthy, sync is in progress. seeing 1,847 buffered orders flowing through now.

messages.text: I see them on our dashboard. all 4 stores are reporting again. thank you both.

text_entities.text: I see them on our dashboard. all 4 stores are reporting again. thank you both.

messages.text: you're welcome — really sorry about the disruption. we'll send you a postmortem by friday and a service credit on the next invoice.

text_entities.text: you're welcome — really sorry about the disruption. we'll send you a postmortem by friday and a service credit on the next invoice.

messages.text: appreciated. one ask: can you also flag what we should look for on our side next time so we know whether to ping you immediately?

text_entities.text: appreciated. one ask: can you also flag what we should look for on our side next time so we know whether to ping you immediately?

messages.text: yes — if your dashboard shows >5 minutes of consecutive 'offline' on more than 2 hoppers, that's almost always us. ping immediately, it'll never be a false alarm.

text_entities.text: yes — if your dashboard shows >5 minutes of consecutive 'offline' on more than 2 hoppers, that's almost always us. ping immediately, it'll never be a false alarm.

messages.text: hey — got a billing q. our invoice for april shows a 15% over-quota fee but we're tracking 11k events under the 12k limit. can you take a look?

text_entities.text: hey — got a billing q. our invoice for april shows a 15% over-quota fee but we're tracking 11k events under the 12k limit. can you take a look?

messages.text: looking now. one moment.

text_entities.text: looking now. one moment.

messages.text: found it — the buffered orders from yesterday's outage came in as a single burst and tripped our daily-spike rule. that's a billing bug on our end, you should not be charged for that.

text_entities.text: found it — the buffered orders from yesterday's outage came in as a single burst and tripped our daily-spike rule. that's a billing bug on our end, you should not be charged for that.

messages.text: I'll credit the over-quota fee on this invoice and patch the spike rule so it ignores backfill bursts. you should see the corrected invoice within the hour.

text_entities.text: I'll credit the over-quota fee on this invoice and patch the spike rule so it ignores backfill bursts. you should see the corrected invoice within the hour.

messages.text: perfect, thanks tariq.

text_entities.text: perfect, thanks tariq.

messages.text: corrected invoice is sent — just resend, original is voided. credit of $186.40 applied.

text_entities.text: corrected invoice is sent — just resend, original is voided. credit of $186.40 applied.

messages.text: got it. thanks.

text_entities.text: got it. thanks.

messages.text: different topic: we've been asking for csv export on the orders dashboard for a while. any update?

text_entities.text: different topic: we've been asking for csv export on the orders dashboard for a while. any update?

messages.text: yes — it's on the roadmap for q2. our pm is actually writing the spec this week. would you be open to a 15-minute call to walk through what columns and filters matter most to you?

text_entities.text: yes — it's on the roadmap for q2. our pm is actually writing the spec this week. would you be open to a 15-minute call to walk through what columns and filters matter most to you?

messages.text: yes. tomorrow afternoon works, after 3pm pacific.

text_entities.text: yes. tomorrow afternoon works, after 3pm pacific.

messages.text: I'll send a calendar invite for thursday 3:30pm pacific with our pm.

text_entities.text: I'll send a calendar invite for thursday 3:30pm pacific with our pm.

messages.text: great.

text_entities.text: great.

messages.text: talked to your pm earlier — really helpful. she said the export will support all 12 columns we use plus a date-range filter. eta end of may?

text_entities.text: talked to your pm earlier — really helpful. she said the export will support all 12 columns we use plus a date-range filter. eta end of may?

messages.text: yes — she walked me through it after your call. we'll have a beta in two weeks for you to test, public release end of may.

text_entities.text: yes — she walked me through it after your call. we'll have a beta in two weeks for you to test, public release end of may.

messages.text: amazing. happy to be the beta tester.

text_entities.text: amazing. happy to be the beta tester.

messages.text: as promised — postmortem for monday's outage is attached. tldr: a config drift from a deploy on sunday set our queue worker timeout 10x too low. the cascade was the symptom; the root cause was the config drift.

text_entities.text: as promised — postmortem for monday's outage is attached. tldr: a config drift from a deploy on sunday set our queue worker timeout 10x too low. the cascade was the symptom; the root cause was the config drift.

messages.text: we've added a config-drift monitor that pages on-call within 30 seconds of any prod-config change that doesn't match our terraform state. small thing, big effect.

text_entities.text: we've added a config-drift monitor that pages on-call within 30 seconds of any prod-config change that doesn't match our terraform state. small thing, big effect.

messages.text: thanks for the writeup, that's a thorough postmortem. forwarding to our cto.

text_entities.text: thanks for the writeup, that's a thorough postmortem. forwarding to our cto.

messages.text: [forwarded] our cto says 'this is the kind of postmortem I wish more vendors wrote, please thank them'

text_entities.text: [forwarded] our cto says 'this is the kind of postmortem I wish more vendors wrote, please thank them'

messages.text: that means a lot, thank you. and please thank your cto.

text_entities.text: that means a lot, thank you. and please thank your cto.

messages.text: good news — csv export beta is live for your account a week early. log in and you should see the new 'export' button on the orders page. would love your feedback this week.

text_entities.text: good news — csv export beta is live for your account a week early. log in and you should see the new 'export' button on the orders page. would love your feedback this week.

messages.text: wow, fast. trying it now.

text_entities.text: wow, fast. trying it now.

messages.text: first impressions: works, the date filter is exactly what we wanted. small thing — when I export over 50k rows the page hangs for a few seconds before the download starts. is there a progress indicator coming?

text_entities.text: first impressions: works, the date filter is exactly what we wanted. small thing — when I export over 50k rows the page hangs for a few seconds before the download starts. is there a progress indicator coming?

messages.text: good catch. progress bar + 'export in background, we'll email you the link' fallback for >25k rows is on the v2 list. I'll bump it up so it's in v1.

text_entities.text: good catch. progress bar + 'export in background, we'll email you the link' fallback for >25k rows is on the v2 list. I'll bump it up so it's in v1.

messages.text: thanks 🙏

text_entities.text: thanks 🙏

messages.text: another small one — could the export include the 'cancellation_reason' column? we need it for our weekly ops review.

text_entities.text: another small one — could the export include the 'cancellation_reason' column? we need it for our weekly ops review.

messages.text: yes, easy add. it'll be in tomorrow's beta build.

text_entities.text: yes, easy add. it'll be in tomorrow's beta build.

messages.text: tomorrow's beta is now today's beta — cancellation_reason column is in. progress bar lands monday.

text_entities.text: tomorrow's beta is now today's beta — cancellation_reason column is in. progress bar lands monday.

messages.text: verified. perfect.

text_entities.text: verified. perfect.

messages.text: appreciate the responsiveness this week. the outage on monday could have been a brutal start but you turned it around.

text_entities.text: appreciate the responsiveness this week. the outage on monday could have been a brutal start but you turned it around.

messages.text: thank you — that's the loop we try to close every time. have a good weekend.

text_entities.text: thank you — that's the loop we try to close every time. have a good weekend.
